report_name: Phishers Abuse No-Code Platform 'Bubble' to Bypass Email Security Filters

threat_actors.name: Phishers
threat_actors.description: Unnamed attackers abusing the Bubble.io no-code platform to create redirector applications for use in credential harvesting campaigns targeting Microsoft 365 users.
threat_actors.goals: Steal user credentials for services like Microsoft 365, potentially leading to Business Email Compromise (BEC) and data breaches.

threat_actors.name: Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) operators
threat_actors.description: Operators of Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platforms who are expected to adopt the Bubble.io abuse technique to automate and scale their attacks. These operators often integrate Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) capabilities to bypass MFA.
threat_actors.goals: Provide phishing kits and infrastructure to other cybercriminals for financial profit.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Phishing emails, often impersonating Microsoft 365, contain a link that directs victims to a malicious redirector application hosted on the Bubble.io platform.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Developer Utilities Proxy Execution
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abuse the legitimate no-code platform Bubble.io to host redirector applications. This leverages the platform's trusted domain (*.bubble.io) to evade email security filters and proxy the user to a malicious site.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on a user clicking a malicious link within a phishing email, which initiates the redirection chain through the Bubble.io application to the final credential harvesting page.

attack_patterns.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
attack_patterns.description: The report notes that Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) kits, which are likely to adopt this Bubble.io technique, often include Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) capabilities to steal session cookies.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
attack_patterns.description: As part of an Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) attack, PhaaS kits can steal session cookies to bypass multi-factor authentication after a user has been phished.

tools.name: Bubble.io
tools.description: A legitimate no-code development platform abused by attackers to create and host malicious redirector applications for phishing campaigns.

identities.name: Microsoft 365
identities.description: The service and its users are the primary target of the credential harvesting phishing campaigns described in the article.

identities.name: Bubble.io
identities.description: The legitimate no-code platform whose services and reputation are abused by attackers to host phishing redirectors, causing reputational damage.

infrastructure.name: Bubble.io Phishing Redirector
infrastructure.description: A malicious web application created on the Bubble.io platform that serves as a redirector, forwarding victims from a trusted domain to an attacker-controlled credential harvesting site.

infrastructure.name: Credential Harvesting Landing Page
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled website, often a pixel-perfect replica of a legitimate login page like Microsoft 365, designed to capture user credentials.

indicators.name: Web Access to Bubble.io Subdomain
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Access to Suspicious Bubble.io Subdomain
id: 9b8c3d5a-1f2e-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects web traffic to bubble.io subdomains. This platform is being abused by phishers to host redirectors that bypass email security filters. While not all traffic is malicious, it can be an indicator of a phishing attempt, especially when followed by a quick redirect to another domain.
references:
  - https://www.kaspersky.com/blog/bubble-phishing-scams/49887/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of applications hosted on the Bubble.io platform.
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith: '.bubble.io'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web traffic to *.bubble.io domains, which are being abused for phishing redirects. Legitimate traffic to Bubble apps exists, so this should be used as a low-confidence indicator or correlated with other suspicious activity.
